title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' features Will Arnett in a leading role, directed by Bradley Cooper, streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' follows Tommy Shelby during WWII, available on Netflix starting March 2026.
HBO Max premieres the bowling docuseries 'Born to Bowl', narrated by Liev Schreiber, in March 2026.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora remains non-committal about his future with the Red Sox as his contract extends through the 2027 season.
Cora, who signed a three-year extension in 2024, focuses on team performance while considering his family's needs for future decisions.
Entering the 2026 season, Cora needs 134 wins to tie Terry Francona's Red Sox managerial win record of 744 games.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins' second line of Viktor Arvidsson, Pavel Zacha, and Casey Mittelstadt has outscored opponents 35-16 this season.
The line's +19 goal differential is second in the NHL, only trailing Colorado Avalanche's top line at +21.
Pavel Zacha leads the line with a career-best 23 goals and 51 points in 66 games this season.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support MGH Pediatric Cancer Center.
This is Mahr's second marathon with MGH; he was inspired by witnessing young patients and survivors in 2014.
Mahr emphasizes the critical need for funding pediatric cancer research, which receives less than adult cancer research.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts voters could see a proposal to reduce income tax rates from 5% to 4% on the November ballot.
The proposed income tax cut is estimated to reduce state revenues by $5.1 billion annually, saving the median household $1,250.
Gov. Maura Healey opposes the tax cut, citing potential risks to state services and economic competitiveness.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church stands trial for allegedly poisoning her boyfriend, Leroy Fowler, with antifreeze due to a love triangle.
Prosecutors presented evidence of ethylene glycol in Fowler's system and a Powerade bottle with the toxic liquid.
Church claims she was venting in notes and filmed Fowler for his substance abuse, not intending to harm him.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposes lifting Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban and expanding crossbow use for all hunters.
The proposed bill aims to better manage deer populations, with MassWildlife's support after public consultation.
Mass Audubon and state officials back the changes, citing environmental benefits and alignment with other states.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Magnet fisherman Nate DeMontigny found a pristine derringer pistol in a Bridgewater river, shared in a YouTube video.
The derringer, found in a case, looked brand new despite being submerged for decades, thanks to stainless steel construction.
The find gained significant online attention, with DeMontigny's post receiving 1.2 million views on a subreddit within two days.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos, 18, charged with murder after a stabbing in Chelsea left one dead and others injured.
Chelsea police responded to the stabbing at 6:45 p.m. near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue on Thursday night.
The deceased victim, a 25-year-old man, died after surgery from multiple stab wounds; other victims' details remain undisclosed.

title: Two officers injured in struggle with knife-wielding man at Forest Hills station, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two officers were injured during a struggle with a knife-wielding man at the Forest Hills MBTA station.
The suspect attempted to grab an officer's weapon, and an accidental discharge occurred, but no one was hit.
The incident led to temporary closure of the upper busway; regular bus service resumed at 1:30 p.m.
